action.reasoning: Observation: Training consistently fails with ModuleNotFoundError for xgboost (code in model.py/train.py/predict.py is otherwise ready; best prior MAE=0.2188 from RF on Morgan FP r=3). MLP on same features was worse (0.231). No eval submitted in last 3 iters due to this import error. Analysis: xgboost is not pre-installed but allowed via packages_to_install (persists); XGB typically beats RF on high-dim sparse FP regression (boosted trees handle it well, expected MAE ~0.16-0.19). FP computation dominates time but was acceptable before (~4min/iter). Adding early_stopping on val set will prevent overfitting and improve val MAE. Decision: Install xgboost, incrementally edit model.py (update XGB params: n_est=500, depth=8, lr=0.05, eval_metric=mae), train.py (add eval_set + early_stopping_rounds=50, use best model for val_mae, update logging), keep predict.py; run training on full data then submit_eval for improved score + timing measurement.
